name: No Name Sandwich
tagline: Word-of-mouth banh mi shop on Waialae — the name tells you everything about the vibe.
mustOrder: classic banh mi — proper baguette, pickled veg, cilantro, jalapeño
try whatever daily special is on the board
talkStory: A Kaimuki banh mi shop that literally has no name on its original signage — regulars just called it 'that sandwich place on Waialae.' Run by a Vietnamese family since the early 2010s, the baguettes are baked fresh and the classic banh mi with pate, pickled daikon, and cilantro rivals anything in Chinatown at half the wait. The prices have barely moved in a decade. The daily specials board is handwritten and often features items not on the regular menu — ask what's fresh.
